result.stdout:
dotnet-tarball

Usage:
  dotnet-tarball [options] [<project>] [command]

Arguments:
  <project>  The project file to operate on. If a file is not specified, the command will search the current directory for one.

Options:
  runtime, -r <runtime>              Target runtime of the tarball. The target runtime has to be specified in the project file.
  framework, -f <framework>          Target framework of the tarball. The target framework has to be specified in the project file.
  configuration, -c <configuration>  Target configuration of the tarball. The default for most projects is 'Debug'.
  output-dir, -o, --output <output>  The output directory to place built packages in. The default is the output directory of your project.
  version-suffix <version-suffix>    Defines the value for the $(VersionSuffix) property in the project.
  --no-restore <no-restore>          Do not restore the project before building.
  -v, --verbose <verbose>            Enable verbose output.
  --version                          Show version information
  -?, -h, --help                     Show help and usage information

Commands:
  install
